SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WINDS KNOCKED DOWN NUMEROUS TREES NEAR THE PRAIRIE PORTAGE AREA OF BASSWOOD LAKE RIGHT ON THE BORDER WITH CANADA. TREES FELL DOWN NEAR THE CAMPSITE OF 9 CAMPERS...PART OF A GROUP AFFILIATED WITH THE BOY SCOUTS OF AMERICA. ONE WOMAN...39 YEARS OF AGE...AND ONE BOY...13 YEARS OF AGE...WERE KILLED WHEN TREES FELL ON THEM. TWO OTHERS WERE INJURED WITH NON-LIFE-THREATENING INJURIES. THE TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
THE WINDS BLEW DOWN A 100 YARD WIDE SWATH OF TREES JUST SOUTH OF ALBORN NEAR HIGHWAY 7. THE TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A TREE FELL DOWN ONTO THE WIRES FOR A 150 FEET TALL RADIO TOWER...WHICH PULLED DOWN THE TOWER. THE TOWER CRASHED ONTO PART OF A HOME AND CAUSED DAMAGE.

HEAVY DAMAGE TO THE ROOF STRUCTURE OVER THE GAS PUMPS AT THE HOLIDAY GAS STATION NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ARROWHEAD AND RICE LAKE ROADS IN DULUTH. THE TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN ON ROADS.

THE DULUTH BOARD OF TRADE BUILDING IN DOWNTOWN DULUTH WAS DAMAGED BY SEVERE WINDS. THE CHIMNEY CRASHED THROUGH THE ROOF OF THE SEVENTH FLOOR. THE DAMAGE ALSO AFFECTED THE STORAGE ROOM FOR COSTUMES FOR THE MINNESOTA BALLET. THE ANEMOMETER ON TOP OF THE NEARBY COUNTY GOVERNMENT BUILDING MEASURED GUSTS TO AROUND 100 MPH. THE TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

THE ALGOMA GUARDIAN...A SHIP 729 FEET LONG...WAS MOVING THROUGH THE DULUTH SHIP CANAL WHEN SEVERE WINDS STRUCK. THE SHIP'S ANEMOMETER MEASURED A GUST OF 103 MPH. THE CREW HAD DIFFICULTY MAINTAINING ITS COURSE THROUGH THE NARROW CANAL AND AVOID HITTING THE BARRIERS ON THE SIDES OF THE CANAL. THE AERIAL LIFT BRIDGE OPERATOR SAID THE BRIDGE'S ANEMOMETER MEASURED A GUST AS HIGH AS 80 MPH...BUT IT'S ANEMOMETER WAS MORE SHELTERED THAN THE SHIP'S ANEMOMETER.
